message:
test(e2e): scope Scroll-to-Explore overlap assertion to hero section and avoid networkidle timeout

Two refinements to `_diag-scroll-indicator.spec.ts` driven by a false
positive and a flake on barra-factor-model.

1. Scope the chip selector to the hero section.

   The previous selector — `div.inline-flex.rounded-full[class*="font-mono"]`
   scanned the entire document. For articles whose hero has no stat
   chips (e.g. barra-factor-model only has a category pill at the top
   of the hero), the selector instead matched a chip deep inside the
   article body (e.g. "LEAST-SQUARES SOLVER" near line 329 of the main
   content), yielding an absurd `chip.bottom ≈ 10071` and a bogus
   assertion failure even though the hero layout was fine.

   Now the selector is scoped to `[data-section="hero"]`. Articles
   without chips inside the hero simply skip the overlap assertion
   (the `info.chip` branch falls through) and just emit the screenshot
   for visual review.

2. Replace `networkidle` with `load` + a short settle delay.

   `barra-factor-model` has background network activity (likely
   canvas/WebGL assets or analytics) that never goes idle within the
   60 s budget, so the spec was timing out before ever reaching the
   assertion. `waitUntil: "load"` + `waitForTimeout(2500)` is enough for
   hydration and animation settle without relying on network silence.

All five articles (wills-and-estate, slack-mattermost, overprompting,
raptorq, barra-factor-model) now pass the spec at 375×800.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
